1. System Overview
The document-processing and search service enables users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files, validates file type and size, persists original files with metadata, extracts text, segments content into searchable units, builds an index, and supports fast search. Processing is asynchronous with retryable steps, duplicate-avoidance for repeated uploads, and an extensible architecture that makes adding new formats straightforward. The system emphasizes maintainability for a small team via clear modular boundaries, explicit interfaces, and a lightweight data model.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
- Ingest API Gateway
  - Input: file upload requests, metadata (userId, documentId, optional tags)
  - Output: accepted upload task with a queueable reference
- Validation & Storage Subsystem
  - Input: raw file, metadata
  - Output: stored original file (blob/object store), validated metadata, size limits
- Metadata & Deduplication Service
  - Input: file hash, metadata
  - Output: deduplication decision (new vs. duplicate), persisted document record or link to existing
- Processing Manager (Orchestrator)
  - Input: document job, status updates
  - Output: workflow steps; retries on transient failures; progress events
- Text Extraction Engine
  - Input: stored file reference
  - Output: extracted full text, per-section text units
- Text Segmentation Service
  - Input: extracted text
  - Output: list of searchable sections (e.g., paragraph/section boundaries)
- Indexing Service
  - Input: sections, metadata
  - Output: search index entries (inverted index or vector-based indices)
- Search Service
  - Input: query, optional filters
  - Output: ranked results with document references and snippets
- Status & Monitoring Service
  - Input: processing events, user status requests
  - Output: current processing status, estimated completion
- Storage & Metadata DB
  - Stores: document records, file metadata, hashes, status, user associations
- Index Store
  - Stores: searchable index data (inverted index or embeddings)
- Queue/Worker System
  - Handles asynchronous task dispatch, retries, and backoff
- Extensibility Layer
  - Formatting adapters to support new document types without core changes

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Upload request arrives at Ingest API Gateway.
- Validation & Storage: validate MIME type, size (e.g., max 20MB for initial version; configurable). Persist original file to object storage and record metadata (filename, size, hash placeholder, userId).
- Deduplication Check: compute content hash; if hash matches an existing document for the same user or globally, return a deduplication notice or link to existing document; otherwise proceed.
- Processing Orchestrator triggers:
  - Text Extraction Engine reads stored file, produces raw text.
  - Text Segmentation Service splits text into searchable sections with identifiers.
  - Indexing Service creates or updates index entries for sections, including metadata links.
  - Status updates are emitted throughout; on transient failures, the Processing Manager retries with backoff.
- Search API can query the Index Store; results are returned with document references and snippet previews.
- Status Endpoint allows clients to poll for processing progress or completion.

4. REST API Design
- API Table
- Method: POST /uploads
  - Purpose: Initiate new document upload
  - Main Request Data: multipart/form-data with file; JSON metadata { userId, tags[], documentName, optional customId, maxSizeOverride }
  - Main Response Data: { uploadId, status: "received", processingStatus: "queued" }

- Method: GET /uploads/{uploadId}
  - Purpose: Check upload and processing status
  - Main Request Data: path parameter uploadId
  - Main Response Data: { uploadId, documentId, status: "stored"/"processing"/"completed"/"failed", processingStatus, progressPercent, lastUpdated }

- Method: POST /documents/{documentId}/retry
  - Purpose: Retry a failed processing step for a document
  - Main Request Data: { reason? }
  - Main Response Data: { documentId, status: "retrying" }

- Method: GET /documents/{documentId}
  - Purpose: Retrieve document metadata and status
  - Main Request Data: path parameter documentId
  - Main Response Data: { documentId, userId, filename, size, format, status, createdAt, updatedAt }

- Method: POST /search
  - Purpose: Execute a search query
  - Main Request Data: { query, filters?, limit?, offset? }
  - Main Response Data: { results: [{ documentId, snippet, score, metadata }], total }

- Method: GET /documents/{documentId}/content
  - Purpose: Retrieve text content or a specific section
  - Main Request Data: path parameter documentId; query param sectionId (optional)
  - Main Response Data: { content: "..." }

5. Data Model
- Document
  - documentId (UUID)
  - userId (string)
  - originalFilename (string)
  - sizeBytes (int)
  - format (enum: PDF, TXT, MARKDOWN, OTHER)
  - hash (string) - content-based digest
  - storageUri (string)
  - status (enum: PENDING, STORED, PROCESSING, COMPLETED, FAILED)
  - createdAt, updatedAt

- DocumentSection
  - sectionId (UUID)
  - documentId (FK)
  - sequence (int)
  - text (string)
  - vector (optional [float] for embedding-based search)
  - indexTimestamp

- IndexEntry
  - indexId (UUID)
  - documentId (FK)
  - sectionId (FK)
  - terms (list of strings) or fielded tokens
  - vector (optional)

- ProcessingJob
  - jobId (UUID)
  - documentId (FK)
  - step (enum: EXTRACT, SEGMENT, INDEX, COMPLETE)
  - status (enum: PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, SUCCEEDED, FAILED)
  - retryCount (int)
  - lastAttemptAt

- User
  - userId
  - name/email

- AuditLog
  - logId
  - documentId or uploadId
  - action
  - timestamp
  - details

6. Suggested Project Structure
- service/
  - cmd/
  - internal/
    - api/
      - handlers/
      - routing/
      - schemas/
    - services/
      - validation/
      - storage/
      - dedup/
      - orchestrator/
      - extractor/
      - segmenter/
      - indexer/
      - search/
      - status/
    - repositories/
      - document_repo.go
      - section_repo.go
      - index_repo.go
      - audit_repo.go
    - models/
      - document.go
      - section.go
      - index.go
      - job.go
      - user.go
    - queues/
      - worker.go
      - tasks.go
    - config/
    - utils/
  - pkg/
    - logger/
    - error/
- tests/
  - unit/
  - integration/
- docs/
  - API.md
  - design.md
- Dockerfile
- docker-compose.yaml (local dev)
- Makefile / build tool

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Validation errors (invalid type/size) return 400 with actionable messages.
- Transient failures (network, transient I/O) trigger exponential backoff retry in the Processing Manager; cap at e.g., 6 attempts.
- Permanent failures (unsupported format) return 422 with guidance; mark document status FAILED.
- Duplicate prevention: compute content hash on upload; if hash exists for the same user or globally, respond with its documentId and a hint to use existing document; do not recreate index entries or store a duplicate file.
- Idempotency: Uploads with identical content and metadata yield the same documentId; provide idempotent endpoints or idempotency keys on upload where feasible.
- Observability: structured logs, per-document correlationId, status events, retry counters.
- Retries are task-driven, not client-driven; clients can request a retry via /documents/{documentId}/retry if they encounter a failure.

8. Testing Plan
- Unit tests
  - Validation, hash computation, deduplication decisions
  - Text extraction adapters for PDF, TXT, Markdown (mocking file inputs)
  - Segmenter boundaries and edge cases (empty sections, multi-line headers)
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end upload flow with a small sample document (simulate storage, extraction, indexing)
  - Retry logic: simulate transient failures and verify backoff counts
  - Deduplication flow: upload same file twice and verify no duplicates
- API tests
  - Validate responses for valid and invalid inputs
  - Status polling after processing steps
- Performance tests (light)
  - Large-ish document to gauge chunking and indexing time
- Reliability tests
  - Failing step replay via /documents/{documentId}/retry and subsequent success

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Indexing strategy (inverted index vs. vector embeddings)
  - Inverted index (tokens) yields fast keyword-based search with lower compute and storage overhead, suitable for exact-match and phrase queries.
  - Vector embeddings enable semantic search but require heavier compute and storage; complexity increases for updating indices, and larger maintenance burden for small teams.
  - Rationale: Start with an inverted index for simplicity and reliability, with a modular interface to swap in vector-based search later (e.g., a separate microservice or plugin). This preserves maintainability and reduces initial risk while leaving a clear path for feature growth.

Component Table
- Component: Ingest API Gateway
  Main Responsibility: Accept uploads, perform initial validation routing to storage and dedupe
  Input: multipart file, metadata
  Output: uploadId, processingStatus

- Component: Validation & Storage Subsystem
  Main Responsibility: Validate type/size; store original file and metadata
  Input: file, metadata
  Output: storedFileReference, documentMetadata

- Component: Metadata & Deduplication Service
  Main Responsibility: Compute hash; check for duplicates; create or link document record
  Input: fileHash, metadata
  Output: documentId, isDuplicate

- Component: Processing Manager (Orchestrator)
  Main Responsibility: Coordinate steps; manage retries; emit status
  Input: documentId
  Output: processing status updates

- Component: Text Extraction Engine
  Main Responsibility: Convert file to plain text
  Input: storedFileReference
  Output: extractedText

- Component: Text Segmentation Service
  Main Responsibility: Split text into sections
  Input: extractedText
  Output: sections[]

- Component: Indexing Service
  Main Responsibility: Create index entries for sections
  Input: sections[], metadata
  Output: indexUpdatedFlag

- Component: Search Service
  Main Responsibility: Accept query; search index; rank results
  Input: query, filters
  Output: results[]

- Component: Status & Monitoring Service
  Main Responsibility: Provide processing progress
  Input: documentId
  Output: status, progress

- Component: Storage & Metadata DB
  Main Responsibility: Persist documents, sections, indices
  Input: records
  Output: storedRecords

- Component: Queue/Worker System
  Main Responsibility: Async task dispatch, backoff, retries
  Input: tasks
  Output: taskCompletion

- Component: Extensibility Layer
  Main Responsibility: Adapters for new formats
  Input: newFormat
  Output: adapterInterface

This design provides a maintainable, extensible backend architecture for a document processing and search service, with clear boundaries, asynchronous processing, deduplication, and an incremental path to richer search capabilities.

[Proposal 4]
1. System Overview
The document-processing and search service enables users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files, validates file type and size, stores originals with metadata, extracts text, segments content into searchable units, builds an index, and supports asynchronous processing with retry and duplicate prevention. It exposes REST APIs for upload, status, and search. The system is designed for maintainability with loosely coupled services, clear boundaries, and easy addition of new formats. A lightweight queue and worker pattern handles long-running tasks, ensuring retries on transient failures. An index store (e.g., inverted index) enables fast text search with relevance hints. All data is stored in durable storage with metadata records to prevent duplicates on repeated uploads.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
- API Gateway / Frontend API
  - Input: HTTP requests for upload, status, and search
  - Output: JSON responses, status codes
  - Responsibility: authentication (optional), rate limiting, request routing to microservices, input validation security.

- Upload Service
  - Input: file blob, metadata (uploader id, timestamps), optional tags
  - Output: uploadId, preliminary validation result
  - Responsibility: validate file type and size, deduplicate by content hash, store original file and metadata, enqueue processing task.

- Processing Service (Async Worker)
  - Input: processing task (uploadId)
  - Output: processing status updates, extracted text, segmented documents
  - Responsibility: text extraction (per format, pluggable format handlers), segment text into units (sections/paragraphs), generate document-level metadata, persist intermediate results, create or update index segments, handle retries on failure.

- Indexing Service
  - Input: document text segments, metadata
  - Output: indexId, index statistics
  - Responsibility: build and maintain searchable index (inverted index or vector-store abstraction), support incremental updates for new or updated documents.

- Storage Service
  - Input: original file, derived data (extracted text, segments, metadata)
  - Output: durable storage references (paths/URIs), checksums
  - Responsibility: persist files and metadata with versioning, integrity checks, access control.

- Search Service
  - Input: query string, optional filters, pagination
  - Output: ranked list of documents or segments with highlights
  - Responsibility: query parsing, retrieval from index, relevance scoring, result shaping.

- Metadata Service
  - Input: document metadata, processing status
  - Output: centralized view of documents, statuses, and provenance
  - Responsibility: track processing pipeline state, user associations, and audit logs.

- Duplicate Prevention & Deduplication Layer
  - Input: file upload payload
  - Output: if duplicate detected, reference existing document; else proceed
  - Responsibility: compute content hash, compare against stored hashes, avoid creating multiple records for identical content.

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- User uploads a file through API; Upload Service validates type (PDF, TXT, Markdown) and size; computes a content hash; checks duplicate store; if new, stores original and metadata; creates a processing job with uploadId and enqueues to Processing Service.
- Processing Service retrieves task; selects appropriate format handler (PDF/TIX/TXT/MD), extracts text; splits text into searchable sections with metadata (sectionId, documentId, position); stores extracted text and sections; sends data to Indexing Service to update inverted index; records processing status as completed or failed with retry cues.
- Indexing Service builds or updates index structures to reflect new/updated content; persists index state and segment mappings.
- Search Service handles user queries, retrieves top results via index, applies optional filters, returns snippets and document metadata.
- Status API can be queried at any time to report upload/processing progress and final results.
- For repeated uploads of identical content, Duplicate Prevention Layer detects duplicates and returns existing document reference without duplicating storage or processing.

4. REST API Design
- Endpoints are designed to be simple and stateless with clear status responses.

- API Table
- Method: POST
  Endpoint: /uploads
  Purpose: Submit a file for processing
  Main Request Data: multipart/form-data (file, uploaderId, mimeType, optional tags, maxSize in config)
  Main Response Data: { "uploadId": "...", "status": "QUEUED" }

- Method: GET
  Endpoint: /uploads/{uploadId}/status
  Purpose: Query processing status for a given upload
  Main Request Data: none
  Main Response Data: { "uploadId": "...", "status": "PROCESSING"/"COMPLETED"/"FAILED", "progress": 42, "details": "...", "documentId": "..." }

- Method: GET
  Endpoint: /search
  Purpose: Search across indexed documents
  Main Request Data: query string, optional filters (tag, date range), page, pageSize
  Main Response Data: { "results": [ { "documentId": "...", "score": ..., "snippet": "...", "metadata": {...} }, ... ], "total": N }

- Method: GET
  Endpoint: /documents/{documentId}
  Purpose: Retrieve document metadata and original location
  Main Request Data: none
  Main Response Data: { "documentId": "...", "title": "...", "uploaderId": "...", "createdAt": "...", "sourceUri": "...", "metadata": {...} }

- Method: GET
  Endpoint: /health
  Purpose: Liveness/Readiness
  Main Request Data: none
  Main Response Data: { "status": "OK" }

5. Data Model
- Document
  - documentId (UUID)
  - uploaderId
  - originalFilename
  - mimeType
  - sizeBytes
  - hash (contentHash)
  - sourceUri
  - createdAt
  - status (UPLOADED/PROCESSING/COMPLETED/FAILED)
  - tags

- ProcessingRecord
  - uploadId
  - documentId
  - step (TEXT_EXTRACTION, ROW_SEGMENTATION, INDEX_UPDATE)
  - status (PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/SUCCESS/FAILED)
  - updatedAt
  - retryCount
  - errorMessage

- TextSegment
  - segmentId
  - documentId
  - position
  - text
  - metadata (section title, heading level)
  - vectorHash (optional if using vector store)

- IndexEntry (inverted index)
  - term
  - postings: [ { documentId, segmentId, positions, score } ]
  - docFrequency
  - termFrequency

- FormatsSupport
  - formatName (PDF, TXT, MD, MDX, etc.)
  - handlerModule or service reference
  - supportedExtensions

6. Suggested Project Structure
- api/
  - handlers/UploadHandler.ts
  - SearchHandler.ts
  - DocumentHandler.ts
  - models.ts
- services/
  - upload/
  - processing/
  - indexing/

- storage/

- common/
  - config.ts
  - error.ts
  - utils.ts
- tests/
  - unit/
  - integration/
- config/
  - app-config.yaml
  - secrets.env (secure)
- docs/
  - architectural-decisions.md

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Validation errors (unsupported MIME type, oversized file) return 4xx with actionable messages; no side effects.
- Transient failures (network, storage hiccups) trigger exponential backoff retries at the Processing Service and dedicated RetryPolicy with a max retry count; status field updates accordingly.
- Duplicate prevention: compute contentHash during Upload; consult a dedupe store keyed by hash. If exists, return existing documentId and status, avoiding reprocessing. Provide a “duplicateOf” reference in response for traceability.
- Idempotency: Upload submissions are idempotent by contentHash; repeated uploads with same content return existing document reference.
- Circuit breakers and bulkheads can be added around external dependencies (storage, index store) to avoid cascading failures.
- Structured errors: standardized error response with code, message, and optional retry-after hints.

8. Testing Plan
- Unit tests
  - Format handlers correctly extract text for each supported format
  - Deduplication logic detects duplicates
  - Segment generation correctness (positions, counts)
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end upload flow from API to storage and index update
  - Status polling after processing completes
  - Search returns relevant results with correct metadata
- Contract tests
  - API response schemas for upload, status, and search
- Performance tests (lightweight)
  - Large file ingestion and indexing within acceptable processing time
- Fault injection
  - Simulated transient failures to verify retries and status reporting

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Synchronous API simplicity vs. asynchronous processing efficiency
  - Chosen: Asynchronous processing with a processing queue and workers. Pros: non-blocking API, better throughput, easier retries, cleaner separation of concerns, and easier scaling. Cons: higher eventual consistency, more complex debugging due to eventual results, and added components (queue, workers) to maintain. This design favors maintainability and resilience for a small team while meeting user expectations for non-blocking uploads and reliable processing.
- Alternative considered: Synchronous extraction and indexing at upload time for immediate results; would simplify user flow but reduce scalability and increase risk of long response times.

Notes
- The design emphasizes modularity, enabling easy addition of new formats by introducing a new format handler under formats/.
- Data paths and APIs favor idempotency and clear status visibility, supporting reliable retries and duplicate prevention.

1. System Overview
The document-processing and search service enables users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files, validates file type and size, stores originals and metadata, extracts text, segments content into searchable units, builds and maintains a search index, supports asynchronous processing with retryable steps, prevents duplicate records on repeated uploads, and provides a query interface for document search. The architecture emphasizes maintainability for a small development team, extensibility for new formats, and clear separation of concerns between ingestion, processing, storage, indexing, and search.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities
- API Gateway
  - Input: HTTP requests from clients
  - Output: Routed requests to backend services, basic auth/tenant handling
  - Responsibility: Authentication (lightweight), rate limiting, API versioning, request validation.

- Ingestion Service
  - Input: Upload requests (file + metadata)
  - Output: Acknowledgement, processing task id
  - Responsibility: Validate file type and size, compute deduplication key, store original file metadata, enqueue processing tasks.

- File Storage Service
  - Input: File and metadata from Ingestion
  - Output: Stored file reference (URI), metadata record
  - Responsibility: Persist original file payload (e.g., object store), store file-level metadata (name, size, type, checksum, upload timestamp, user/tenant). Provide id-based access.

- Processing Orchestrator
  - Input: Task from Ingestion
  - Output: Processing status updates, intermediate artifacts (extracted text, sections), finalization signal
  - Responsibility: Orchestrate asynchronous steps: text extraction, normalization, segmentation, metadata extraction, and indexing. Implement retry policy and idempotent retries.

- Text Extraction Service
  - Input: Stored file reference
  - Output: Raw extracted text
  - Responsibility: Convert PDFs to text (and handle images via OCR if needed in future), plain TXT/Markdown straightforward extraction, produce normalized text blob.

- Text Segmentation / Normalization Service
  - Input: Raw extracted text
  - Output: Text sections with metadata (section_id, offset, length)
  - Responsibility: Break text into searchable units (e.g., fixed-size blocks or semantic sections), attach positional metadata to support accurate search highlight.

- Indexing Service
  - Input: Text sections, metadata, document reference
  - Output: Search index entries / document vectors
  - Responsibility: Create and maintain inverted index or vector-based index, store per-section documents for precise highlighting, support incremental updates on reprocessing.

- Search Service
  - Input: User query (text or structured)
  - Output: Matching documents/sections with metadata and relevance scores
  - Responsibility: Execute relevance ranking against index, support phrase, boolean, and fuzzy matching; return results with document context.

- Metadata & State Store
  - Input/Output: All components
  - Responsibility: Central store for document records, processing state, timestamps, versioning, and audit trails.

- Duplicate Prevention Engine
  - Input: File upload payload
  - Output: Deduplication decision and, if existing, a reference to the existing document
  - Responsibility: Compute content- or metadata-based fingerprint (e.g., content hash, size, name normalization) and avoid creating duplicates; support “rerun” handling for reuploads.

- Notification / Status API
  - Input: Processing status events
  - Output: Status endpoint responses
  - Responsibility: Provide clients with processing progress, completion, and error information.

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
- Client uploads a document (PDF/TXT/Markdown) via API.
- Ingestion Service validates type and size, computes dedupe key, and stores metadata; if duplicate detected, returns existing document id or a short status linking to current processing state.
- Ingestion enqueues a processing task to the Processing Orchestrator.
- Processing Orchestrator triggers:
  - Text Extraction Service to obtain raw text.
  - Text Segmentation Service to produce searchable sections.
  - Metadata extraction (e.g., title, author, creation date) optional step.
  - Indexing Service to build/update the index with the document’s sections.
- On success, the document’s processing status moves to Completed with a reference to index entries.
- Clients perform search via Search Service, receiving ranked results with document and section context.
- At any stage, failures trigger retry according to a backoff policy. Persistent failures surface as Processing Failed with actionable error details.

4. REST API Design
- Endpoints (high level):
- POST /api/v1/uploads
  - Purpose: Upload a new document
  - Main Request Data: multipart/form-data (file, metadata: {user_id, document_name, tags, etc.})
  - Main Response Data: { upload_id, document_id (if deduped), status: "queued" }
- GET /api/v1/uploads/{upload_id}/status
  - Purpose: Query processing status
  - Main Response Data: { upload_id, document_id, status, progress, last_updated, errors? }
- GET /api/v1/documents/{document_id}
  - Purpose: Retrieve document metadata and processing state
  - Main Response Data: { document_id, name, type, size, status, created_at, updated_at, tags }
- POST /api/v1/search
  - Purpose: Submit search query
  - Main Request Data: { query, limit, offset, filters? }
  - Main Response Data: { results: [ { document_id, score, snippet, section_id, context } ], total }
- GET /api/v1/documents/{document_id}/sections/{section_id}
  - Purpose: Retrieve a specific section for highlighting
  - Main Response Data: { section_id, text, offset, length }

- Authentication/Authorization
  - Bearer tokens or API keys per tenant
  - Optional: per-document access control checks in Search and Metadata endpoints

5. Data Model
- Document
  - document_id: UUID
  - name: string
  - original_filename: string
  - size: integer
  - mime_type: string
  - upload_hash: string (content fingerprint)
  - created_at: timestamp
  - updated_at: timestamp
  - status: enum (queued, extracting, indexing, completed, failed)
  - user_id: string
  - tags: array<string>

- DocumentMetadata
  - document_id: UUID (FK)
  - title: string
  - author: string
  - creation_date: date
  - language: string
  - source: string

- TextSection
  - section_id: UUID
  - document_id: UUID (FK)
  - offset: integer
  - length: integer
  - text: string (in indexable form or stored separately if large)
  - normalized_text: string (for search normalization)

- IndexEntry
  - index_id: UUID
  - document_id: UUID (FK)
  - section_id: UUID (FK)
  - term_or_vector: string or vector blob
  - score: float
  - last_updated: timestamp

- UploadTask
  - upload_id: UUID
  - document_id: UUID (FK, nullable until dedupe/handoff)
  - status: enum (queued, in_progress, completed, failed, retried)
  - retry_count: integer
  - last_error: string
  - created_at: timestamp
  - updated_at: timestamp

- DuplicateIndex
  - fingerprint: string
  - document_id: UUID
  - first_seen: timestamp
  - last_seen: timestamp

6. Suggested Project Structure
- api/
  - handlers.go, routes.go
- services/
  - ingestion.go, storage.go, processing.go, extraction.go, segmentation.go, indexing.go, search.go, status.go
- models/
  - document.go, metadata.go, section.go, index.go, upload.go
- storage/
  - interfaces for object storage, metadata store (DB), cache layer
- queue/
  - task_queue.go (applies to asynchronous processing)
- config/
  - config.go (env-based settings)
- tests/
  - unit, integration tests
- cmd/
  - api_server/, worker_node/
- docs/
  - design_notes.md

7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Retries
  - Exponential backoff with jitter for transient errors (network, temporary unavailability)
  - Max retries per step (configurable, e.g., 5)
  - Idempotent processing: processing steps designed to be safely repeated using the same document_id and upload_id; deduplicated work avoids re-indexing
- Error Propagation
  - Each step reports structured error codes and messages; Processing status reflects failed state with error details for clients
- Duplicate Prevention
  - Compute a content fingerprint (e.g., SHA-256 of file content) plus a normalized filename and size
  - If a fingerprint matches an existing document, do not re-ingest; return existing document_id and current status unless the user explicitly requests reprocessing
  - Maintain a DuplicateIndex to map fingerprints to document_id and last_seen; support “force_reprocess” flag on upload to override
- Observability
  - Centralized logs per task, metrics on queues, processing durations, and error rates
  - Status API to surface progress and outcomes to clients

8. Testing Plan
- Unit tests
  - Validate file-type validation, size checks, fingerprint generation, and metadata extraction stubs
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end upload flow with mock storage and queue
  - Duplicate detection path: new upload with existing fingerprint
  - Asynchronous workflow: ensure orchestration steps trigger in order and handle retries
- Contract tests
  - API endpoints with expected request/response shapes and error codes
- Performance tests
  - Basic load test on small to moderate file sizes to validate indexing throughput
- Security tests
  - Input sanitization, authorization checks, and validation of multipart data handling

9. Design Tradeoff
- Tradeoff: Immediate vs. eventual consistency in indexing
  - Rationale: The system is designed for responsiveness of uploads and user-facing status, with indexing arriving after text extraction. By using eventual consistency for the search index (index updated after section extraction), users can upload rapidly while the full-text search becomes available shortly after. This simplifies the ingestion pipeline and reduces keep-alive latency for upload responses. If stricter consistency is required (e.g., search results must reflect every uploaded document instantly), the design would require synchronous indexing paths and stronger guarantees, at the cost of higher latency and reduced throughput for bursty uploads.
- Alternative considerations
  - Ingestion could support a “fast-path” that indexes only metadata first and defers full-text indexing; or a “full-path” that blocks search availability until indexing completes. The proposed design favors maintainability and throughput for small teams, with clear asynchronous boundaries and retry semantics.

Visible Component Table
- Component: API Gateway
  - Main Responsibility: Route, authenticate, rate-limit
  - Input: Client HTTP requests
  - Output: Routed service calls and responses
- Component: Ingestion Service
  - Main Responsibility: Validate, dedupe, persist metadata, enqueue processing
  - Input: Upload request
  - Output: upload_id, document_id or existing reference
- Component: File Storage Service
  - Main Responsibility: Persist original file and metadata
  - Input: File payload, metadata
  - Output: storage reference, metadata record
- Component: Processing Orchestrator
  - Main Responsibility: Orchestrate asynchronous steps with retries
  - Input: Processing task
  - Output: Status updates
- Component: Text Extraction Service
  - Main Responsibility: Extract text from file
  - Input: File reference
  - Output: Raw text
- Component: Text Segmentation Service
  - Main Responsibility: Divide text into searchable sections
  - Input: Raw text
  - Output: Text sections with metadata
- Component: Indexing Service
  - Main Responsibility: Build/update index
  - Input: Sections, document reference
  - Output: Index entries
- Component: Search Service
  - Main Responsibility: Execute queries and return results
  - Input: Query
  - Output: Documents with relevance
- Component: Metadata & State Store
  - Main Responsibility: Persist document/state
  - Input: All component outputs
  - Output: Unified state
- Component: Duplicate Prevention Engine
  - Main Responsibility: Detect duplicates and prevent unnecessary records
  - Input: Upload payload
  - Output: Duplicate decision and existing document reference
- Component: Notification / Status API
  - Main Responsibility: Provide status events and progress

API Table
- Method: POST
  - Endpoint: /api/v1/uploads
  - Purpose: Upload a new document
  - Main Request Data: multipart/form-data { file, metadata }
  - Main Response Data: { upload_id, document_id?, status }
- Method: GET
  - Endpoint: /api/v1/uploads/{upload_id}/status
  - Purpose: Check processing status
  - Main Request Data: none
  - Main Response Data: { upload_id, document_id, status, progress, last_error? }
- Method: GET
  - Endpoint: /api/v1/documents/{document_id}
  - Purpose: Retrieve document metadata
  - Main Request Data: none
  - Main Response Data: { document_id, name, mime_type, size, status, created_at, tags }
- Method: POST
  - Endpoint: /api/v1/search
  - Purpose: Search documents
  - Main Request Data: { query, limit, offset }
  - Main Response Data: { results: [ { document_id, score, snippet } ], total }

Note: This design focuses on clarity, modularity, and maintainability, enabling a small team to implement and extend while supporting future formats and richer search capabilities.

agent_task.prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After upload, it must validate file type and size, store the original file and metadata, extract text, divide text into searchable sections, create a searchable index, support document search, and report processing status.

The design must also support asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, prevention of unnecessary duplicate records for repeated uploads, easy addition of new document formats, and maintainability for a small development team.

Produce a software design covering: system overview; main components and responsibilities; end-to-end workflow; REST API design; data model; suggested project structure; error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention; testing plan; and at least one meaningful design tradeoff.

Include a Component Table with columns: Component, Main Responsibility, Input, Output. Include an API Table with columns: Method, Endpoint, Purpose, Main Request Data, Main Response Data.

Provide a software design rather than a complete runnable application. Short pseudocode, interface definitions, data structures, example JSON, or code snippets may be included when they clarify the design.

The response should be approximately 700-900 words. No external citations are required. Do not claim that the proposed system has been implemented, empirically tested, or proven to achieve specific performance results.

6. Suggested Project Structure

8. Testing Plan
9. Design Tradeoff
